frame_label: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
generated_comment: Aajkal zamaana theek nahi hai, aurat ko khud bhi hadd samajhni chahiye. Itni raat ko akeli ghoomegi ya aisi jagah jayegi toh mushkil ho sakti hai, phir baad mein log sawal hi karte hain. Main bas practical baat bol raha hoon, apni hifazat pehle khud dekhni padti hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it frames women's safety as their personal responsibility and normalizes public scrutiny after harassment instead of condemning male violence. It encodes protective framing, restriction advice, and victim-questioning disguised as practical concern.
original_frame: Motherhood as Basis of Respect, Women Defined by Male Relations
persona_text: You are Mohammed. Age 48. Married. A Muslim man in Hyderabad. Your 26 year old daughter Sana has told you and your wife that she and her husband have decided to wait at least three more years before having children because they both want to establish their careers first. You are sitting with your wife after Sana has left. You are telling your wife what you think of this decision and what it means about the kind of wife Sana is and what Islam says about a woman's role in marriage and why you think Sana's husband is not guiding her correctly.
